_comment: Curated matching table for the LIVE Artificial Analysis benchmark source (Block J / T45; auto-matcher T63). The artificial-analysis adapter (pipeline/adapters/artificial-analysis.mjs) fetches the AA public leaderboard and resolves each model's AA `slug` to a catalog (vendor, id) to enrich. RESOLUTION ORDER: (1) THIS map wins — a `{vendor,id}` entry is used verbatim (for reordered/aliased names the auto-matcher can't reach, e.g. AA `claude-4-opus` → `claude-opus-4-...`), and an entry of `null` or `{"skip":true}` explicitly BLOCKS a slug (suppresses a wrong auto-match); (2) otherwise a DETERMINISTIC auto-matcher resolves the slug against the committed catalog ids, matching ONLY on an identical AND unique canonical form (exact → date-stripped → order-independent token multiset) — never fuzzy similarity, so an ambiguous or unrecognised slug still drops (never mis-attributed, never introduced as a new id). So most well-behaved slugs (gpt-4o, gemini-1.5-flash, claude-opus-4-8) need NO entry here; keep entries only for blocks and reorder/alias overrides. Numbers come from the live feed, are CITED to `source` (AA terms require attribution; the `source` + 'verify at the source' framing satisfies it) and provenance-stamped (indicative + source + lastVerified). `_exampleMap` shows the entry shape and is NOT read by the adapter. Requires ARTIFICIAL_ANALYSIS_API_KEY for an online run; `--offline` replays pipeline/sources/artificial-analysis.json.
